behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct total for A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit (SUM arithmetic). Second pass: failure can occur at parser -> range resolver -> function dispatch handoff, so Unit can miss boundary bugs (MOCK-MISS). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required for this behavior; no AI output; no multi-step agent pipeline, so not System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to known numbers, set a target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run full engine evaluation, assert computed value equals expected sum.

behaviors.description: Cross-cell cascaded formula evaluation (e.g., C1=`=B1+1`, B1=`=A1*2`) is correct.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Re-check against Unit: behavior explicitly depends on cross-cell formula handoff, so single-function tests are insufficient (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System because behavior text is about local evaluation correctness, not OS/network/subprocess effects (REJECTION-GATE).
behaviors.test_description: In one sheet, set A1=5, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert B1=10 and C1=11.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency detection returns a circular-reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Second pass considered System (runtime hang timing), but disqualified: behavior text is dependency-graph cycle detection in formula references, testable in-process by evaluator/graph modules. Unit is too low because cycle emerges across multiple cells/modules.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`; run evaluation with a bounded step/time guard; assert engine reports circular-reference error type/code and does not produce a value.

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact match returns expected value; missing key returns lookup error.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit (lookup algorithm). Second pass: behavior is formula-engine level (`VLOOKUP` in cells), so parser/reference coercion + function execution interaction is the likely real-failure boundary. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range in cells, evaluate a `VLOOKUP(..., FALSE)` formula for an existing key and a missing key, assert correct matched value and correct not-found error respectively.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions evaluate correct branch outputs.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit. Second pass against Integration: behavior text is conditional-expression semantics, and failure can be isolated within expression evaluation logic without cross-module infrastructure. Rejected Integration because no required module handoff beyond one evaluator boundary is stated (REJECTION-GATE).
behaviors.test_description: Call expression/formula evaluator on `IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` with table-driven A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Unit. Second pass: this is function-level string assembly/empty-value semantics; no required real infra or multi-module orchestration in the behavior itself. Rejected Integration due lack of explicit cross-module failure boundary text.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test CONCATENATE evaluation with inputs containing empty cells and separators; assert output omits empties (no extra blank segments) and preserves separator placement for non-empty values.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Re-check: failure occurs at sheet namespace resolution + evaluator handoff, not a single local function. Rejected System because no OS/network/subprocess behavior is required by this correctness statement.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1=41, set Sheet1!B1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate, assert Sheet1!B1=42.

behaviors.description: Dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order after a referenced value changes.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Second pass confirms: this is graph scheduling + dependency propagation across modules; Unit on one function can miss ordering/propagation defects. Rejected System: behavior text does not require external process/OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 base, B1/C1 depend on A1, D1 depends on B1 and C1), evaluate once, mutate A1, trigger recalculation, assert all downstream values match expected recomputation based on dependency order.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: Integration. Re-check: failure boundary is interaction of edit history/state snapshots with recalculation engine; Unit is too low for this handoff. Rejected System because behavior is internal state restoration, not OS/network/subprocess-specific.
behaviors.test_description: Set formulas with dependents, evaluate baseline, edit an upstream formula and recalc, perform undo, assert upstream formula text is restored and all dependent computed values return to baseline.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and load back with identical formulas/values and no floating-point precision drift.
behaviors.justification: Provisional: System (real disk wording). Second pass: core failure target is serializer/deserializer fidelity; this is catchable with local file round-trip inside process. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require OS-specific edge conditions (locks, watcher events, crashes), only data fidelity after save/load.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with representative formulas and floating-point values, save to a temp file via normal persistence API, reload via load API, assert exact formula text equality and value equality within strict precision tolerance (or bit-exact if format guarantees it).
